name: Chicken with 40 Cloves of Garlic
recipeIngredient: 1 Large Frying or Roasting Chicken (3 1/2 pounds)
Sprigs of Fresh Rosemary or Thyme (optional)
2 Tablespoons Fresh Basil or Parsley, minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken thoroughly and pat it dry with paper towels.
2. Rub the skin of the chicken with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
3. Mix together the rub ingredients (Dry Herb Rub) and rub evenly over the chicken, including the cavity.
4. Place a lemon quarter in the cavity along with herb sprigs if used.
5. Refrigerate the chicken and marinate for at least 2 hours.
6. In an ovenproof casserole dish large enough to hold the chicken, add the remaining olive oil and garlic cloves.
7. Sauté over medium heat for 3-4 minutes until the garlic is lightly golden.
8. Add white wine and chicken stock, and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
9. Place the marinated chicken in the casserole dish, cover, and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25 minutes.
10. Increase the temperature to 450°F (230°C) and continue baking uncovered for 40-50 minutes or until juices run clear when tested at the thigh joint. Ensure there is always liquid in the bottom of the casserole; if not, add a little more wine or stock.
11. Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest. Spoon pan juices over the chicken and top with minced basil.
12. Arrange garlic cloves around the chicken.
13. Squeeze garlic onto the chicken as you eat.
Yield: 4 - 6 servings
